senses.definition: A severe lack of will or ability to make decisions, especially as a medical or psychological problem.
examples: The neurologist noted signs of abuleia after the patient’s stroke.
In extreme abuleia, a person may find it hard to start even simple tasks.

senses.definition: A medical condition in which a person has an abnormal lack of will, motivation, or ability to make decisions.
examples: Abulia can appear after damage to certain parts of the brain.
People with abulia may seem indifferent and rarely start activities on their own.

senses.definition: Plural of abulia: several cases or types of abnormal lack of will or motivation.
examples: The study compared different abulias in elderly patients.
Clinicians reported more abulias following frontal lobe injuries.

senses.definition: Showing abulia; having an abnormal lack of will or ability to act.
examples: After the accident he became abulic and rarely spoke.
The patient’s abulic state made rehabilitation very slow.

senses.definition: A mental disorder marked by a great and often painful difficulty in making decisions.
examples: Abulomania can make everyday choices feel overwhelming.
The psychiatrist considered abulomania as part of the diagnosis.

senses.definition: A very rare or obsolete spelling related to abulia, referring to a lack of will or decision.
examples: Older medical texts sometimes mention abulyeit as a condition of weak will.
The character’s abulyeit prevents him from choosing any path.

senses.definition: Related to or like an umbra, especially a dark central shadow, in a very rare or technical way.
examples: The astronomer described an abumbral effect during the eclipse.
Such abumbral regions are difficult to observe directly.

senses.definition: Very rare humorous or dialect form meaning related to or provided with an umbrella.
examples: He invented an abumbrellar hat for rainy days.
The abumbrellar device popped open at the first drop of rain.

senses.definition: A title used for a bishop or patriarch in certain Eastern Christian churches, especially the Ethiopian Orthodox Church.
examples: Abuna Paulos served as the head of the Ethiopian Orthodox Church.
The faithful gathered to welcome the new Abuna after his appointment.

senses.definition: Plural of abuna: several bishops or patriarchs with the title Abuna.
examples: Historically, different abunas played important roles in church politics.
The book describes the lives of three notable abunas.

senses.definition: A very large quantity of something.
examples: The garden produced an abundance of fresh vegetables.
There is an abundance of information available online.

senses.definition: Plural of abundance: several large quantities or levels of something.
examples: Different regions of the country have different abundances of wildlife.
The study measured the abundances of various chemical elements in the rocks.

senses.definition: Plural of an old‑fashioned form abundancy, meaning large amounts of something.
examples: Older texts speak of the abundancies of nature.
The poet praised the abundancies of God’s blessings.

senses.definition: An old‑fashioned word for abundance, meaning a large amount of something.
examples: He wrote of the abundancy of crops after the rains.
Such abundancy of detail is typical of writers of that era.

senses.definition: Existing in very large quantities; more than enough.
examples: Fish are abundant in this part of the river.
There is abundant evidence that climate is changing.

senses.definition: The Latin name Abundantia, often used for a Roman goddess of plenty or as a poetic name for abundance.
examples: In Roman mythology, Abundantia was associated with prosperity and grain.
The poet invoked Abundantia to bless the harvest.

senses.definition: In a way that is more than enough; in great quantity.
examples: The room was abundantly lit by large windows.
Water is abundantly available in this region.

senses.definition: Used for emphasis, meaning very clearly or very much.
examples: It is abundantly clear that changes are needed.
She made her position abundantly plain.

senses.definition: A Scottish form of above, meaning higher than or over, used mainly in dialect or poetry.
examples: The birds were singing abune the trees.
He held his head abune the water and swam to shore.

senses.definition: A tropical African tree whose wood or oil is used commercially.
examples: The carpenter chose abura for its light color and workability.
Abura is sometimes used as a substitute for more expensive hardwoods.

senses.definition: In Japanese folklore, a spirit or monster that appears in steam, especially in baths.
examples: The story tells of an aburabozu rising from the hot springs.
Children were warned that an aburabozu might appear if they stayed in the bath too long.

senses.definition: A Japanese name for certain oil‑producing trees, such as the tung tree, used for wood oil.
examples: Traditional craftsmen used oil from aburagiri to finish wood.
Aburagiri plantations once covered the hillsides.

senses.definition: A very rare humorous blend suggesting a mix of absurd and urban in style or character.
examples: The film’s aburban setting mixed city life with surreal comedy.
Her fashion sense is delightfully aburban and playful.

senses.definition: Suddenly and in a burst, especially with laughter, sound, or movement.
examples: The crowd broke aburst into cheers when the team scored.
She ran aburst from the house, unable to hide her excitement.

senses.definition: A rare family name or place name; used chiefly as a proper name.
examples: Dr. Aburton presented the findings at the conference.
They drove through a small village called Aburton.

senses.definition: Able to be abused or used in a wrong or harmful way.
examples: Power is always abusable if it is not controlled.
Some medications are highly abusable and tightly regulated.

senses.definition: Incorrect, awkward, or bad use of language.
examples: The book is full of notes on common abusage in English.
Teachers corrected students’ abusage in their essays.

senses.definition: Plural of abusage: several examples or types of incorrect language use.
examples: The manual lists common abusages to help learners avoid mistakes.
Such abusages are typical in informal online writing.

senses.definition: Cruel, violent, or unfair treatment of a person or animal.
examples: The organization helps victims of domestic abuse.
Child abuse is a serious crime in every country.

senses.definition: Rude or insulting language.
examples: The players received abuse from the crowd.
Online abuse can seriously harm a person’s mental health.

senses.definition: Wrong or harmful use of something, especially power, drugs, or substances.
examples: There were several cases of power abuse in the company.
Long‑term alcohol abuse can damage the liver.

senses.definition: To treat someone in a cruel, violent, or unfair way.
examples: He abused his children and was sent to prison.
Animals are often abused in illegal dog‑fighting rings.

senses.definition: To use something in a wrong or harmful way.
examples: Some politicians abuse their power for personal gain.
He abused the system to receive extra benefits.

senses.definition: To speak to someone in a very rude and insulting way.
examples: They abused the referee throughout the match.
She was abused online after expressing her opinion.

senses.definition: Treated in a cruel, violent, or unfair way, especially over a period of time.
examples: She works with abused children.
The documentary told the story of abused women seeking help.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of abuse.
examples: He abused his position to gain money.
They admitted they had abused the animals.

senses.definition: In a way that involves abuse or is wrongly and badly used, in rare or old‑fashioned use.
examples: Power, so abusedly held, soon collapses.
He spoke abusedly to his workers and lost their respect.

senses.definition: A person who is the victim of abuse, used rarely and mainly in technical or advocacy contexts.
examples: Services must be designed with the needs of the abusee in mind.
Each abusee in the study received counseling and legal support.

senses.definition: Humorous or nonstandard blend meaning both abusive and useful at the same time.
examples: Her comments were oddly abuseful, but they did help him improve.
The tool is abuseful if used roughly, yet it solves the problem fast.

senses.definition: In a way that is both abusive and, in a joking sense, useful.
examples: He spoke abusefully, yet his advice was accurate.
The coach shouted abusefully but pushed the team to win.

senses.definition: A humorous or rare idea of being both abusive and useful.
examples: The abusefulness of his comments made people laugh and think.
Writers joked about the abusefulness of strict teachers’ methods.

senses.definition: A person who treats someone in a cruel, violent, or unfair way.
examples: Survivors of domestic violence often fear their abuser.
The abuser was finally arrested and charged.

senses.definition: A person who uses something, especially drugs or alcohol, in a harmful or excessive way.
examples: He is a long‑term drug abuser.
Alcohol abusers are at high risk of liver disease.

senses.definition: Plural of abuser: people who abuse others or use things in a harmful way.
examples: Abusers are often people the victim already knows.
The law aims to punish drug abusers and protect public health.

senses.definition: Plural of abuse: several acts of cruel treatment, rude language, or wrong use of something.
examples: The report documented serious human‑rights abuses.
Over time, small abuses of power can damage trust.

senses.definition: Third person singular of abuse: he, she, or it treats someone badly, uses something wrongly, or speaks very rudely.
examples: He abuses his position to get special favors.
She abuses anyone who disagrees with her online.

senses.definition: Nonstandard or humorous form of ambush, meaning to attack by surprise.
examples: They planned to abush their friends with water guns.
The children tried to abush their teacher at the door.

senses.definition: Present participle of abuse: treating someone cruelly, using something wrongly, or speaking very rudely.
examples: He was accused of abusing his employees.
They are abusing the system to claim extra money.

senses.definition: An old‑fashioned word for abuse or wrongful practice.
examples: The reformers spoke out against the abusion of church power.
Such abusion could no longer be tolerated.

senses.definition: Plural of abusion: several abuses or wrongful practices, in old‑fashioned use.
examples: He listed the many abusions of the old system.
Political abusions led to public anger.

senses.definition: Very rare old‑fashioned word meaning full of abuse or using abuse.
examples: His abusious speech shocked the audience.
The debate became abusious and unproductive.

senses.definition: Using cruel, violent, or unfair treatment toward someone.
examples: She left her abusive partner and found a safe place to live.
Abusive relationships can be hard to escape.

senses.definition: Using very rude and insulting language.
examples: The fans were abusive toward the referee.
He sent her a series of abusive messages online.

senses.definition: In a way that is cruel, violent, or very rude toward someone.
examples: He spoke abusively to the staff and was asked to leave.
People who behave abusively online may be banned from the platform.

senses.definition: The quality of being cruel, violent, or very rude, especially in the way you treat or speak to others.
examples: His abusiveness eventually drove his friends away.
The abusiveness of their relationship was clear to everyone.

senses.definition: Plural of abusiveness: different kinds or examples of cruel or very rude behavior.
examples: The report described various abusivenesses in the workplace.
Different abusivenesses were addressed through training and policy changes.

senses.definition: To be next to something or to share a border or edge with it.
examples: Their garden abuts the school playground.
The two fields abut each other along the river.

senses.definition: A tropical climbing plant whose roots are used in traditional medicine.
examples: In some regions, abuta is used as a traditional remedy.
Researchers studied the possible medical uses of abuta extract.

senses.definition: Any plant of the Abutilon genus, often grown for its colorful, hanging flowers.
examples: They planted abutilon along the garden wall for its bright blooms.
The abutilon will flower from late spring to early autumn.

senses.definition: Plural of abutilon: several plants of the Abutilon genus.
examples: Abutilons are popular in mild climates for their long flowering season.
Different abutilons can be combined to create a colorful border.
